blockingIssues.description: The root landing still renders the hero before the atlas, so the default visible state is hero-first rather than a pure graph workspace as required by VAL-ROADMAP-014 and VAL-CROSS-014.
The guided investor path labels a Valuation gate but maps it to the `risks` node, so the ordered path does not actually deliver a valuation surface.
Two visible planned roadmap stages (`ti-data-pipeline-phase2` and `sa-premium-api`) still have no dedicated execution-plan treatment, so not every primary roadmap stage satisfies VAL-ROADMAP-016.
Several proof gates remain concrete but not externally observable, which does not satisfy the falsifiable externally observable proof-gate requirement in VAL-ROADMAP-016.
The constrained/mobile path still falls back to a static hierarchy-preserving background instead of the promised cheaper-motion path.
The deliverable does not include the explicit side-by-side `tradebetter.app` comparison and extracted-theme evidence required for VAL-VISUAL-021.
Recorded validation lacks concrete runtime smoothness and deferred-heavy-layer observations, so the VAL-VISUAL-025 performance claims remain unproven.

appliedUpdates.description: Extended `.factory/library/testing.md` to note that viewport-first assertions such as a default visible graph workspace need browser/visual or layout-aware verification; DOM order checks alone are insufficient.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit CTA-destination evidence for graph-first navigation features before workers can claim `followedProcedure=true`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `investor-graph-workspace-default-and-guided-pitch-path` found the transcript/browser evidence covered scrolling to the atlas and guided-path behavior, but not the required CTA-destination validation, while the handoff still marked procedure adherence as complete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Define how roadmap nodes qualify as `primary` when every primary stage must receive a dedicated execution plan.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `investor-roadmap-execution-plan-and-timing-model` introduced a local `PRIMARY_STAGE_IDS` subset and omitted visible planned nodes `ti-data-pipeline-phase2` and `sa-premium-api`, which left those stages without execution-plan treatment.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten test-first and adherence-reporting guidance so implementation-before-tests sessions cannot still report `followedProcedure=true` without an explicit deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `investor-roadmap-execution-plan-and-timing-model` and `investor-valuation-corridors-and-vault-capacity-model` were reviewed with transcript evidence showing implementation edits before the new rendered-behavior tests, yet both handoffs reported `followedProcedure=true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when tradebetter parity follow-up features may reuse previously extracted theme evidence versus when they must rerun `FetchUrl`/`pinchtab`, and require an explicit committed side-by-side comparison artifact when parity evidence is part of the contract.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `investor-visual-parity-and-balanced-performance-retune` review found reused library context plus fresh browser checks, but no fresh `FetchUrl`/`pinchtab` extraction and no committed side-by-side parity artifact, while the handoff still marked `followedProcedure=true`.

rejectedObservations.observation: The valuation-model review repeated the same `content-strategy-worker` test-first adherence gap already raised by the execution-plan review.
